content.problemStatement:
Utkarsh has a permutation PPP of {0,1,2,…,N−1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,…,N−1}.

He wants to find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1≤L<R≤N)(1 \leq L \lt R \leq N)(1≤L<R≤N) such that swapping the elements PLP_LPL​ and PRP_RPR​ does not change the MEX of any subarray.

Formally, find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1≤L<R≤N)(1\leq L \lt R \leq N)(1≤L<R≤N) satisfying the following condition:


Let P′P'P′ be the permutation defined as follows:

Pi′=PiP'_i = P_iPi′​=Pi​ for i∈{1,2,3,…,N}∖{L,R}i \in \{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N\} \setminus \{L, R\}i∈{1,2,3,…,N}∖{L,R}
PL′=PRP'_L = P_RPL′​=PR​
PR′=PLP'_R = P_LPR′​=PL​


mex({Pi,Pi+1,…,Pj})=mex({Pi′,Pi+1′,…,Pj′})mex(\{P_i, P_{i+1}, \ldots, P_j\}) = mex(\{P'_i, P'_{i+1}, \ldots, P'_j\})mex({Pi​,Pi+1​,…,Pj​})=mex({Pi′​,Pi+1′​,…,Pj′​}) for every 1≤i≤j≤N1 \leq i \leq j \leq N1≤i≤j≤N
For example, let N=7N = 7N=7 and P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]P = [2, 5, 1, 0, 3, 6, 4]P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]. Then,


Swapping the pair (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), i.e swapping P1=2P_1 = 2P1​=2 and P3=1P_3 = 1P3​=1 would give us the permutation P′=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]P' = [1, 5, 2, 0, 3, 6, 4]P′=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]. This changes the mex of the subarray [3,4][3, 4][3,4], because mex({P3,P4})=mex({1,0})=2mex(\{P_3, P_4\}) = mex(\{1, 0\}) = 2mex({P3​,P4​})=mex({1,0})=2 but mex({P3′,P4′})=mex({2,0})=1mex(\{P'_3, P'_4\}) = mex(\{2, 0\}) = 1mex({P3′​,P4′​})=mex({2,0})=1, so (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) is not a valid swap.
Swapping the pair (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) gives us the permutation P′=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]P' = [2, 5, 1, 4, 3, 6, 0]P′=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]. This changes the mex of the subarray [7,7][7, 7][7,7] — mex({P7})=mex({4})=0mex(\{P_7\}) = mex(\{4\}) = 0mex({P7​})=mex({4})=0 and mex({P7′})=mex({0})=1mex(\{P'_7\}) = mex(\{0\}) = 1mex({P7′​})=mex({0})=1. So, (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) is also not a valid pair.
However, it can be verified that the pair (2,6)(2, 6)(2,6) is valid — swapping P2P_2P2​ and P6P_6P6​ gives us the permutation P′=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4]P' = [2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4]P′=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4], and mex(P[i…j])=mex(P′[i…j])mex(P[i \ldots j]) = mex(P'[i \ldots j])mex(P[i…j])=mex(P′[i…j]) for every 1≤i≤j≤71 \leq i \leq j \leq 71≤i≤j≤7.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Description of each test case:
The first line contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​, denoting the permutation PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs (L,R)(L,R)(L,R) such that swapping PLP_LPL​ and PRP_RPR​ does not change the MEX of any subarray.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
It is guaranteed that PPP is a permutation of {0,1,2,…,N−1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,…,N−1}.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are no valid pairs.
Test case 222: There is only one valid pair, (L,R)=(2,6)(L,R) = (2,6)(L,R)=(2,6), i.e, swapping P2=5P_2 = 5P2​=5 and P6=6P_6 = 6P6​=6 to obtain the permutation [2,6,1,0,3,5,4][2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4][2,6,1,0,3,5,4].
